Permanent Skate Park

Permanent Skate Park ¾ Project  Description:

    This  project  includes  designing  and  constructing  a permanent skate park located on the Crummer/Case Court parcel adjacent to Malibu Bluffs Park.  The project would include parking and additional site amenities such as trash cans, benches, tables, and restrooms.

The Planning Commission will then review the project in Summer 2022 before final review by the Council.

Capital Improvement Projects (Not Started)

Vehicle Protection Devices ¾ Project Description:  Per Malibu Municipal Code Section 17.

48.070 requires vehicle impact protection devices to be installed for all parking spaces located adjacent  to  any  outdoor  pedestrian  seating  area.    There  are  two  locations within the City’s right-of-way  and City-owned property that would require a vehicle impact protection device.

PCH Median Improvements at Paradise Cove and Zuma Beach ¾ Project Description:

  This project was identified in the 2015 PCH Safety Study and includes installing new raised medians and improvements.  New raised medians are proposed east and west of PCH and Paradise Cove Road.

ff072d24-033b-43e6-b376-8f250b8b40ba.label: text_chunk

medians on PCH in the areas where the double yellow lines exist in the vicinity of Zuma Beach, specifically where the yellow paddles are installed.

Capital Improvement Projects (Completed)

Civic Center Way Improvements

Updates:  The  contractor  completed  the  project  and  Council  accepted  this  project  as complete at the September 13th City Council Meeting.

Civic Center Stormwater Diversion Structure

Updates: The contractor has completed the storm drain improvements on Civic  Center Way.

07a528ac-70a7-4503-b6f1-4a0a84357c97.label: text_chunk
07a528ac-70a7-4503-b6f1-4a0a84357c97.text:
The improvements modified the existing concrete channel underneath Civic Center Way  by  adding  a  3’  concrete  curb  adjacent  to  the  existing  flap  gates.    The  new  curb prevents  brackish  water  intrusion  into  the  City’s  Civic  Center  Stormwater  Treatment Facility.

 This project was accepted by the Council at the January 24, 2022 meeting.

60d4ca3c-b2bf-457e-96da-ed4f07ae1dce.label: text_chunk
60d4ca3c-b2bf-457e-96da-ed4f07ae1dce.text:
2021 Annual Street Maintenance

Updates:  This  project  included  resurfacing  Malibu  Road,  Broad  Beach  Road,  Latigo Canyon  Road,  Corral  Canyon  Road,  Webb  Way,  Rambla  Pacifico  Street  and  Vista Pacifica with a slurry seal treatment and adding speed humps to Birdview Avenue.  This project was identified in the City’s Pavement Management Plan.

2148df57-904e-46b9-b0e7-9612f7598754.label: text_chunk
2148df57-904e-46b9-b0e7-9612f7598754.text:

Storm Drain Master Plan (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project is funded through a grant from FEMA after the Woolsey Fire.  The City will create a complete inventory of storm drains, culverts, debris basins, manholes, and other drainage structures within the City.

¾ Updates:  Council approved an agreement in December for design services.

Clover Heights Storm Drain (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of design and construction of a few storm drains on Clover Heights.  The existing storm drain facility ends at the intersection  of  Clover  Heights  and  Harvester  Road.    During  storms,  this intersection floods and causes debris to block the road.

b19ab570-8a70-4094-8021-ea27af1c1829.label: text_chunk
b19ab570-8a70-4094-8021-ea27af1c1829.text:
An extended storm drain towards the end of Clover Heights will help eliminate this issue.

¾ Updates:

 An agreement was awarded at the June 13, 2022 Council meeting.

  A kick-off meeting has been scheduled with the Consultant to begin the design process.

Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements (CalOES Project) ¾ Project Description:

  This project consists of repairing damage storm drain facilities and roadway embankments that were damaged by the Woolsey Fire.

¾ Estimated Schedule:  Complete Design: Fall 2022  Begin Construction:

 Spring 2023

Outdoor Warning Sirens - Design (FEMA Project)

¾ Project Description:

  An Emergency Warning Siren system will improve the City’s  ability  to  alert  the  community  by  providing  mechanisms  to  alert residents in areas without power and those who live in neighborhoods with poor  cell  service.

1822004b-3ba7-4345-ac78-088c981ec81d.label: text_chunk
1822004b-3ba7-4345-ac78-088c981ec81d.text:
Phase  One  of  the  project  included  a  sound  study  to determine the optimal number and locations of individual sirens, power and infrastructure  requirements,  and  siren  sound  range.

    The  City  has  been awarded a FEMA Hazard Mitigation grant to fund the design, engineering and environmental compliance needed for a shovel ready project.

ddccb957-9eec-4aab-8b06-f0148aecbbbd.label: text_chunk
ddccb957-9eec-4aab-8b06-f0148aecbbbd.text:
Phase Two of the Emergency Warning Sirens project will be implementation of the design plan,  including  purchasing,  installing,  and  testing  the  sirens.    The  City  will apply for future grant funding for this phase of the project. ¾ Updates:

 On  November  30,  2021,

  City  Council  approved  an  agreement  with Acoustic Technology to design the outdoor warning sirens.

dabeb7b6-25c6-41de-babd-ba4da0aa4072.label: text_chunk
dabeb7b6-25c6-41de-babd-ba4da0aa4072.text:
A design report will be presented to Council upon completion.

 Comments  were  sent  back  to  the  Consultant,

  a  meeting  will  be scheduled to discuss those comments with the consultant in the next few days.

¾ Estimated Schedule: TBD

community_summary.2:
The relationships outlined involve various projects and initiatives related to infrastructure improvements. 

1. **Marie Canyon Green Streets** is a specific project that is part of the **Fiscal Year 2021-2022 Capital Improvement Program**. This indicates that it is funded and relevant within the context of broader capital improvement efforts for that fiscal year.

2. **Clover Heights** is involved in the **Encinal Canyon Road Drainage Improvements** project, where an extended storm drain is being implemented to address drainage issues in the area. This highlights Clover Heights' role in the larger initiative aimed at improving drainage infrastructure.

In summary, both Marie Canyon Green Streets and Clover Heights are integral components of larger capital improvement programs, focusing on enhancing infrastructure and addressing specific community needs.
community_summary.3: The PCH Median Improvements Project is a significant initiative that has received crucial approvals from key regulatory bodies. On September 8, 2021, the Planning Commission approved the project, enabling it to move forward. Additionally, the project obtained approval from Caltrans, as the work is set to take place along the Pacific Coast Highway. These endorsements highlight the project's compliance with necessary regulations and its importance for infrastructure development in the area.
community_summary.4:
The summary of relationships involving the Council is as follows:

1. **Construction Management Services**: The Council formally approved an agreement for construction management services, indicating their endorsement of the necessary services for an upcoming project.

2. **Project Funding**: The Council allocated funding specifically for additional engineering work related to a designated Project, showcasing their financial support for project enhancements.

3. **Right Turn Lane Construction**: The Council awarded a contract for the construction of a right turn lane, reflecting their formal approval of this infrastructure initiative.

4. **HVAC Replacement Project**: The Council awarded the contract for the HVAC Replacement Project, highlighting their role in both approving and funding this essential upgrade.

5. **Civic Center Way Modifications**: During a meeting on January 24, 2022, the Council approved modifications to Civic Center Way, demonstrating their commitment to improving local infrastructure.

6. **Meeting on January 24, 2022**: The Council convened on this date to discuss and accept various projects, emphasizing their active involvement in local governance.

7. **Consultant Design Review**: The Council reviews design reports prepared by consultants prior to making decisions, indicating their thorough approach to project evaluation and oversight.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the Council's active role in approving, funding, and overseeing various infrastructure projects and services within the community.

The relationships involving the City highlight its multifaceted role in managing public infrastructure and responding to community needs. 

1. **Capital Improvements Projects**: The City manages these projects to enhance public infrastructure, ensuring that community facilities and services are improved and maintained.

3. **Vehicle Protection Devices**: The City enforces regulations for the installation of vehicle protection devices, reflecting its responsibility for public safety as outlined in the Malibu Municipal Code.

4. **FEMA Grant**: The City has been awarded a grant from FEMA, establishing a financial relationship aimed at funding hazard mitigation efforts, which underscores the importance of federal support in local disaster preparedness.

5. **Contract with Acoustic Technology**: The City has contracted Acoustic Technology to design outdoor warning sirens, indicating a professional collaboration focused on enhancing emergency communication systems.

6. **Bidding Process**: On April 7, 2022, the City received bids, showcasing its active role in procurement and project management.

7. **Agreement Award**: The City awarded an agreement on June 13, further illustrating its involvement in formalizing contracts and partnerships for various projects.

Overall, these relationships illustrate the City's proactive approach to infrastructure management, disaster recovery, public safety, and collaboration with external entities for community enhancement.
